path._doc: Only 'portable' is honored on sm_121 at CTK 13.0: dequant fp8 -> fp32, fp32 FMA matmul (the op's parity target). The native fp8 MMA (MmaFP8Op, m16n8k32) is gated on CTK >= 13.1; the native sm_121 blockscaled MMA (MmaSM120BlockScaledOp) is MX microscaling (e8m0/e4m3), not this op's DeepSeek fp32 block=128 scales. Accepted for signature parity with the triton card.

perf.regime: Portable dequant-then-fp32-matmul via CUTE DSL on Grace+Blackwell GB10 (sm_121). No matrix engine usable for THIS op on THIS arch today (fp8 MMA gated CTK>=13.1; sm_121 blockscaled MMA is MX not DeepSeek; bf16 MMA fails fp32 rtol=1e-3), so the kernel is a coalesced scalar-fma fp32 GEMM with Kahan-compensated K-reduction. PERF PASSES on ds5 (2026-06-29): (i) ncu profile->diagnose->fix: transposing B to (K,N) for coalesced B[k,n] warp access cut the kernel dispatch 221us->90us (2.5x), Compute SM 18.6%->42.3%, kernel now compute/memory-balanced (~42%/42%). (ii) compile-cache (c SOLVED): @cute.jit __call__ rebuilt the MLIR exec engine EVERY call (~9.3ms, GPU idle ~99%); switched to cute.compile() -> reusable CudaDialectJitCompiledFunction handle cached per (M,N,K) shape, launched with tensors-only (NO constexpr re-passed -> that corrupts the TVM-FFI ABI and segfaults). END-TO-END 9.47ms -> 0.0795ms (119x), tflops 0.01 -> 0.84. End-to-end now ~= ncu kernel-dispatch (90us), so the kernel (not launch) is now the bottleneck -> follow-up (d) is worthwhile.

measured.source: scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_cute_perf.py (xkernels benchmark/do_bench) on ds5 GB10 sm_121, CTK 13.0. END-TO-END incl host dequant+transpose+launch, post compile-cache (c). ncu kernel-dispatch alone: ms=0.090, tflops=0.75, achieved_bw_pct=42.3 (scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_ncu_baseline.py).

notes:
CUTE DSL (`cutlass.cute`, nvidia-cutlass-dsl 4.5.2) backend; first native-CUDA card in the library, validated on the ds5 Grace+Blackwell GB10 test bed (docs/usage/ds5-testbed.md). JIT-compiles via nvcc (CUDA_HOME must be set). Registers only if nvidia-cutlass-dsl imports; else degrades to the triton/reference card. The kernel dequant-then-fp32-FMA path is numerically faithful to mm_fp8_blockscale_ref (dequant both operands to fp32, fp32 matmul).

Correctness (2026-06-29): compiled=true; verify 5/5 sweep points PASS on the GPU (correctness.passed=True) — including the strict fp32 point (rtol 1e-3) and all four bf16 points. The bf16 rtol was CALIBRATED to 0.016 this session (was 1e-2): the prior 1e-2 covered 1 bf16-ULP (2^-7 ~= 7.8e-3) but false-failed the 2-ULP boundary case at bf16 M=128 N=512 K=512 (rel 1.36e-2). That was proven a bf16-mantissa rounding-boundary artifact, NOT a kernel defect: fp32-vs-fp32 agreement vs the torch CUDA matmul reference is max_abs=2.3e-5 (the Kahan-compensated sum is marginally MORE accurate than torch's blocked reduction), and scripts/archive/ds5-probes/m128_bf16_rootcause.py shows EVERY valid fp32 reduction (Kahan/naive/blocked/tree) lands at exactly 1-2 bf16-ULP after the cast. The 0.016 value is the canonical bar — torch.testing's per-dtype default for bfloat16 (torch 2.12, torch/testing/_comparison.py), itself derived from the 7-mantissa-bit width. cross_backend_rtol raised 1e-2 -> 0.02 to stay >= the bf16 single-backend rtol (library.md Sec.5.4 invariant). See registry/ops/mm_fp8_blockscale.spec.json numerics.notes for the full derivation. (Earlier diagnostic: scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_m128_diag.py.)

Parity caveat (honest): verify_parity() hardcodes device='cpu', so it cannot meaningfully test this GPU-only CUTE card. The genuine cross-backend agreement is the GPU fp32-vs-reference max_abs=2.3e-5. Full 3-way GPU parity is gated on (a) restoring Triton on ds5 (missing python3.12-dev) and (b) extending verify_parity to run GPU cards on the GPU device.

PERF PASSES (2026-06-29, ds5 GB10; use-nsight-compute methodology adapted to a bare node — no container/DCGM/sbatch; ncu 2025.3.1 recognizes sm_121). 
(i) COALESCE FIX (profile->diagnose->fix): baseline naive kernel (one thread/output, scalar K-loop over global B[n,k]) — ncu Duration 221us, Compute SM 18.6%, Eligible warps/sched 0.19, dominant stall 67.4cyc (71.6%) L1/LG-memory-queue throttle (the 16-way K-strided B[n,k] access of consecutive-n threads) -> diagnose-memory-bound. FIX (math-preserving 3-line): transpose B to (K,N) host-side for coalesced B[k,n] warp reads. AFTER: Duration 90us (2.5x), Compute SM 42.3% = Memory 42.3% (balanced), LG stall gone; residual latency = Kahan K-chain dependency (scoreboard 11.6cyc).
(ii) COMPILE-CACHE FIX (c, SOLVED — the load-bearing one): @cute.jit __call__ rebuilt the MLIR execution engine EVERY call via compile_and_cache->compiler_provider.jit(module) (~9.3ms, GPU idle ~99%; cold 117ms). Read the DSL source: cute.compile(fn,*args) returns a reusable CudaDialectJitCompiledFunction handle (TVM-FFI, holds the engine, generate_execution_args + run_compiled_program per call ~40us). GOTCHA (cost ~3 probing iterations): the handle specializes on the constexpr (M,N,K) at COMPILE time, so it MUST be launched with ONLY the tensor args — re-passing the constexpr corrupts the TVM-FFI execution-args ABI and SEGFAULTS in the native launch (host_runtime.py documents compiled_fn(*args) but not the no-constexpr rule). Integrated as a per-(M,N,K) handle cache in fp32_matmul_cute. RESULT: end-to-end 9.47ms -> 0.0795ms (119x), tflops 0.01 -> 0.84; correctness unchanged (5/5, fp32-strict pass). End-to-end (~79us) now ~= ncu kernel-dispatch (90us) -> the kernel is now the bottleneck, so follow-up (d) is worthwhile. Probes: scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_c_solved_test.py, scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_c_final_probe.py, scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_jit_source_probe.py, scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_compile_src_probe.py.
(iii) KERNEL ILP (d, NEGATIVE RESULT — documented so the next agent doesn't repeat): a 2-way Kahan (two independent even/odd chains merged at the end) was tried to attack the residual scoreboard stall (11.6 cyc). Per ncu it REGRESSED: scoreboard stall 11.6->15.4 cyc, kernel duration 90->102us (occupancy rose 53->68% but didn't translate to speed). Cause: the single chain is already at its ILP ceiling for this (8x16 tile, one-output-per-thread) shape; the 2-way adds a merge-dependency + register pressure that outweigh the ILP gain. Reverted to single-Kahan (90us, the 42/42 balanced optimum). Conclusion: the scalar fp32-FMA kernel cannot be meaningfully sped up by unrolling at this tile; the ONLY real perf lever is the matrix engine (fp8 MMA, gated on CTK 13.1 on sm_121) or a full static-layout smem-tiled rewrite (separate, high-effort, and a different kernel style). Correctness of the reverted single-Kahan: re-verified 5/5, fp32-strict point passes.

Parity/GPU-device note: a device guard in fp32_matmul_cute raises RuntimeError on CPU tensors so verify_parity (which hardcodes device='cpu') records CUDA as a caught backend error instead of segfaulting on the native launch. Genuine cross-backend agreement remains the GPU fp32-vs-reference max_abs=2.3e-5; full 3-way GPU parity still gated on restoring Triton on ds5 (python3.12-dev) + extending verify_parity to run GPU cards on GPU.
